mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures and signals economic deceleration, causing equity markets to price in lower corporate profit growth and decline over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer spending and aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive mean return over 60 days in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and reduced factory utilization, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations following an INDPRO decline, or the signal shows no predictive power during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, borrowing costs for corporations and households become restrictive enough to suppress capital expenditure and consumption, forcing equity valuations lower over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >60% of observations when the threshold is breached, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect increased construction activity and consumer confidence in durable goods, generating immediate positive sentiment and upward price pressure on the S&P 500 over the following five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations following HOUST increases, or the signal rank correlation with returns is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below its 2017 baseline level, it indicates persistent manufacturing weakness that erodes corporate earnings expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear market outcomes with statistical significance.
